name: Macoupin County Housing Authority
description_short: Non-profit housing authority providing affordable rental housing, homeownership programs, and credit counseling across Macoupin County, Illinois.
description_long:
Macoupin County Housing Authority (MCHA) is a public housing agency serving low and moderate-income residents across Macoupin County, Illinois. Established to provide decent, safe, and sanitary housing, MCHA operates public housing developments in nine communities: Virden, Girard, Palmyra, Carlinville, Gillespie, Benld, Mt. Olive, Staunton, and Bunker Hill. The agency is headquartered in Carlinville with administrative offices at 760 Anderson Street.

MCHA offers three primary service areas: public housing rental programs for income-qualified families, a Family Self-Sufficiency program designed to help residents achieve economic independence, and a Homeownership Center that includes first-time homebuyer programs and credit counseling services. Their credit counseling program is specifically designed to help low-income individuals develop the financial credentials and knowledge necessary to qualify for homeownership. All services are tailored to meet the needs of residents seeking affordable housing solutions and financial stability.

As a government-affiliated housing authority, MCHA distinguishes itself through its multi-faceted approach combining rental assistance, financial education, and homeownership pathways under one organization. The agency conducts regular inspections and maintenance compliance standards, demonstrating accountability to residents and funding sources. Their presence across nine municipalities provides localized service delivery and community-specific housing opportunities.

MCHA is best suited for low to moderate-income individuals and families seeking affordable rental housing or first-time homebuyer assistance in central Illinois. The agency's credit counseling program offers genuine non-profit financial education rather than predatory lending alternatives. Residents should expect professional housing management and compliance with federal public housing standards, though services are geographically limited to Macoupin County.
pros: Provides affordable rental housing across nine Macoupin County communities with varying location options
Offers free or low-cost credit counseling services specifically designed to build homeownership eligibility
Operates as a government housing authority with federal oversight and HUD compliance standards
First-time homebuyer program available alongside rental options for economic mobility
Family Self-Sufficiency program designed to move residents toward long-term financial independence
Conducts regular REAC inspections and maintains housekeeping standards for quality living conditions
Direct phone contact and physical office location in Carlinville for in-person assistance
cons: Services limited to Macoupin County only; no assistance available outside this geographic area
Website content limited; program details and eligibility requirements not comprehensively documented online
No information provided regarding application timelines, wait lists, or current rental availability
Credit counseling program effectiveness and counselor certification details not specified on website
No mention of special programs for homeless populations or veterans
best_for: Low to moderate-income renters seeking affordable housing in Macoupin County communities
First-time homebuyers in central Illinois building credit and financial readiness
Families pursuing self-sufficiency programs and long-term economic stability
Individuals seeking non-profit credit counseling in Illinois
diagnosis: MCHA is ideal for low-income residents and first-time homebuyers in Macoupin County seeking affordable housing and legitimate credit counseling from a government-backed agency. The main limitation is strict geographic service area restriction to Macoupin County, making it unavailable to residents in surrounding regions.
services: Public housing rental programs across nine Macoupin County locations
Family Self-Sufficiency program for economic independence building
Housing unit inspections and maintenance standards enforcement
Work order and maintenance request services
Community events and resident engagement activities
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
